prompt_md: A teammate pastes a customer's full support record (name, email, account ID) into a public chatbot to get a quick summary, and is about to forward the AI's summary — which includes a refund figure — straight to the customer.

decision_choices: Stop: the record shouldn't go to an unapproved tool, and the refund figure must be verified before it reaches the customer
Let it go — it's just a summary and speed matters
Only fix the chatbot choice; the refund figure is fine to send as-is
Only verify the figure; the tool choice doesn't matter
reason_choices: Confidential PII must stay on the approved platform, and any customer-facing number is high-stakes and needs a trusted-source check
Summaries are always safe to forward without checking
Public chatbots are fine as long as the output looks reasonable
AI figures are reliable enough to send to customers without verification
